techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.reason: Public-facing endpoints can be exploited to gain initial access
techniques.mitigation: Deploy WAF, apply OWASP Top 10 controls, and regularly patch internet-facing services

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.reason: External remote services and VPN gateways are common initial access vectors
techniques.mitigation: Restrict external remote access to authorised users via MFA; use zero trust network access

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.reason: Cloud-hosted systems are targeted via credential stuffing and account compromise
techniques.mitigation: Enforce MFA on all accounts; implement Conditional Access policies

techniques.name: Trusted Relationship
techniques.reason: High vendor count increases trusted third-party access paths
techniques.mitigation: Apply least privilege to all vendor integrations; review vendor access quarterly

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.reason: External data sharing increases phishing surface via known email addresses
techniques.mitigation: Enable anti-phishing controls; train staff on social engineering

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.reason: Legacy systems often expose scripting interfaces that can be abused
techniques.mitigation: Restrict scripting capabilities; apply application allowlisting on legacy hosts

techniques.name: Software Deployment Tools
techniques.reason: Numerous integration points increase attack surface for deployment tool abuse
techniques.mitigation: Restrict deployment pipeline access; sign and verify all deployment artefacts

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.reason: Without MFA, attackers can persist by modifying account credentials
techniques.mitigation: Enforce MFA; monitor for account creation or modification events

techniques.name: Server Software Component
techniques.reason: Public-facing web servers can be compromised and backdoored
techniques.mitigation: Apply file integrity monitoring; restrict write access to server directories

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.reason: Unpatched legacy software is a common target for privilege escalation exploits
techniques.mitigation: Maintain regular patching cadence; implement endpoint privilege management

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.reason: Non-zero-trust architectures may allow elevation through misconfigured roles
techniques.mitigation: Apply least privilege principles; review IAM role assignments regularly

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.reason: Absence of monitoring allows attackers to disable defenses undetected
techniques.mitigation: Implement centralised SIEM with tamper-evident log storage

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.reason: Without monitoring, attackers can remove forensic evidence undetected
techniques.mitigation: Send logs to immutable, off-host storage in real-time; enable audit logging

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.reason: Public-facing services without MFA are vulnerable to brute force attacks
techniques.mitigation: Enable account lockout policies; require MFA on all public-facing authentication

techniques.name: Credentials from Password Stores
techniques.reason: Systems handling credentials are targeted for extraction from password stores
techniques.mitigation: Use a secrets manager (Azure Key Vault); avoid storing credentials in code or configs

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.reason: Large integration footprint without zero trust allows lateral discovery
techniques.mitigation: Implement network segmentation; enforce micro-perimeter controls

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.reason: Flat legacy networks allow adversaries to enumerate accessible systems
techniques.mitigation: Apply zero trust network segmentation; restrict ICMP and port scanning capabilities

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.reason: Without zero trust controls, adversaries can move laterally via remote services
techniques.mitigation: Require re-authentication for remote service access; apply just-in-time access

techniques.name: Remote Service Session Hijacking
techniques.reason: Multiple vendor remote sessions increase session hijacking risk
techniques.mitigation: Use PAM solutions with session recording; rotate session tokens frequently

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.reason: Cloud-hosted sensitive data is targeted for unauthorised collection
techniques.mitigation: Apply storage ACLs and private endpoints; enable storage access logging

techniques.name: Data from Information Repositories
techniques.reason: Systems processing PII are targeted for bulk data collection
techniques.mitigation: Implement DLP controls; restrict bulk data export capabilities

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.reason: External data sharing paths can be abused for data exfiltration
techniques.mitigation: Inspect and restrict outbound traffic; apply egress filtering and DLP

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.reason: Cloud-hosted public services can be used as exfiltration channels
techniques.mitigation: Monitor outbound data flows for anomalies; apply CASB controls

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.reason: Unencrypted data stores are vulnerable to destructive attacks
techniques.mitigation: Enable encryption at rest; implement immutable backup strategies

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact (Ransomware)
techniques.reason: Legacy systems are frequently targeted in ransomware campaigns
techniques.mitigation: Maintain offline/immutable backups; segment legacy systems from production networks

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.reason: Public endpoints without WAF protection are vulnerable to DoS attacks
techniques.mitigation: Deploy DDoS protection (Azure DDoS Standard) and WAF in front of public endpoints

techniques.name: Drive-by Compromise
techniques.reason: Engineering workstations accessing the internet can be compromised via drive-by downloads
techniques.mitigation: Restrict internet access from OT engineering workstations; apply application allowlisting

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.reason: OT/SCADA systems with remote access capabilities are prime targets
techniques.mitigation: Broker all remote access through a dedicated secure remote access solution (e.g. Claroty SRA)

techniques.name: Spearphishing Attachment
techniques.reason: OT staff are targeted via spearphishing to gain initial access
techniques.mitigation: Enable email security controls; conduct OT-specific security awareness training

techniques.name: Command-Line Interface
techniques.reason: CLI access to OT systems can be exploited to execute malicious commands
techniques.mitigation: Restrict CLI access; log all command execution; require PAM session management

techniques.name: Execution through API
techniques.reason: OT APIs with many integrations can be used to execute unauthorised commands
techniques.mitigation: Apply strict API authentication and authorisation for OT system APIs

techniques.name: Default Credentials
techniques.reason: OT devices frequently shipped with default credentials that are never changed
techniques.mitigation: Enforce credential change procedures for all OT devices; use PAM to vault credentials

techniques.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
techniques.reason: Legacy OT remote services often have unpatched vulnerabilities
techniques.mitigation: Apply compensating controls (firewall, IDS) around legacy OT remote services

techniques.name: Loss of Availability
techniques.reason: OT/SCADA systems are targeted to cause loss of operational availability
techniques.mitigation: Implement redundancy and BCDR plans; test OT recovery procedures regularly

techniques.name: Manipulation of Control
techniques.reason: Attackers can manipulate control systems to cause physical process disruption
techniques.mitigation: Implement IEC 62443 zone and conduit model; apply data diodes at OT/IT boundary

techniques.name: Theft of Operational Information
techniques.reason: OT operational data is valuable intelligence for adversaries planning physical attacks
techniques.mitigation: Apply need-to-know controls on OT data; restrict data export from OT boundary
